mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 1% for an extended period signals persistent monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span after a period of stability, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations are revised downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered-return is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional average returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in housing starts and retail sales reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength; the joint signal is more predictive of near-term equity gains than either series alone because it confirms demand momentum is not confined to a single sector.
failure_condition: Joint-positive signal produces forward 20d returns no better than the univariate HOUST-only or RSAFS-only signal, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not incrementally positive versus the additive baseline (delta IC < 0.01) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of stable-to-rising industrial production growth in a low-rate monetary regime, short-horizon equity momentum is reinforced because production gains confirm corporate revenue durability without raising cost-of-capital concerns, supporting positive 1-week forward returns.
failure_condition: Hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum in a sub-1% FEDFUNDS regime show a hit rate below 50% over any rolling 2-year sample, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, implying a negative real policy rate, equity markets tend to rally over the following month as cheap real financing conditions incentivize risk-taking and multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL % change < 0 (negative real rate)
failure_condition: Falsified if forward 20d returns conditioned on negative real rates are not significantly different from the unconditional distribution (p > 0.05) across two or more non-overlapping historical sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor demand and household income growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rejected if the sign of trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth has no measurable relationship (Rank IC < 0.02) with forward 20d SPX returns over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by unemployment near cyclical lows, decelerating retail sales growth signals waning consumer demand momentum that is not yet reflected in equity prices, leading to below-average S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as growth expectations reprice downward.
failure_condition: Hypothesis fails if retail sales deceleration during low-unemployment regimes is not followed by below-median 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances across the historical sample, or if the effect reverses sign outside late-cycle regimes without being flagged as regime-dependent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in industrial production growth and payroll employment growth signals an emerging synchronized supply-side and labor-side contraction that markets have not fully priced, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the very short term as leading indicators jointly deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < 0 AND PAYEMS 3-month change < prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Falsified if joint deterioration signal shows a hit rate for negative 5-day forward returns below 50% over any 2-year rolling window, or if the interaction signal underperforms the single-series INDPRO-only trigger by more than 0.02 in Rank IC.
